channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: In promising to shake up Washington, Trump is in a class of his own
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In the history of American politics, there's no shortage of presidents who promised to shake up Washington once they got to the White House.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Golden Globe nominations are Monday. Here's what to look for
item.description: Expect a starry field of actors when nominations to the 82nd Golden Globes are announced Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: "Upon arrival, the students and staff were declined service and asked to no longer include the restaurant on its CBI list. The group was able to place a carry-out order," Navarro's letter read.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, NewsNation

item.title: Cracker Barrel 'refused service' to students with disabilities, Maryland school says
item.description: Cracker Barrel has issued an apology after a Maryland school district said a group of students with disabilities was denied service at one of the chain's restaurants.
item.dc:creator: Skyler Sales, WGN 9

item.title: 10 hospitalized after motorcycle officer crashes into crowd at California parade
item.description: A crash involving a motorcycle officer left at least 10 people hospitalized during a holiday parade in Palm Springs, California, Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Scottie Scheffler ends his big year in the Bahamas with his 9th victory
item.description:
Scottie Scheffler returned from a two-month break and nothing changed. He won the Hero World Challenge on Sunday with a 9-under 63 to tie the tournament record at Albany Golf Club and win by six shots.
Scheffler, the first player since Tiger Woods in 2009 to spend an entire calendar year at No.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 10 hospitalized after officer crashes into crowd during Palm Springs holiday parade
item.description: The officer, who was reportedly performing a wheelie trick, suddenly lost control of his bike and fell off, witnesses told the Desert Sun.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he can't guarantee tariffs won't raise US prices and won't rule out revenge prosecutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump said he can't guarantee that his promised tariffs on key U.S. foreign trade partners won't raise prices for American consumers and he suggested once more that some political rivals and federal officials who pursued legal cases against him should be imprisoned.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Woman, 52, accused of robbing Billie Eilish fans at knifepoint near Oregon arena
item.description: A woman was arrested for allegedly stabbing a man, then threatening and robbing a couple of their sleeping bag near the Moda Center Saturday morning.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, KREX 5

item.title: US journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared in Syria in 2012, believed to be alive: Biden
item.description: “We think we can get him back," Biden told reporters at the White House, while acknowledging that “we have no direct evidence” of his status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Forever chemicals tainting food supply, destroying American farmers
item.description: Advocates like Erin Brockovich are sounding the alarm. She says when it comes to forever chemicals, “the storm is here.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, Fox 5

item.title: Why work-life balance isn't working for women
item.description: About half of working women reported feeling stressed “a lot of the day," compared to about 4 in 10 men, according to a Gallup report published this week.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: LA Galaxy strike early, hold off New York Red Bulls 2-1 to win their record 6th MLS Cup championship
item.description: CARSON, Calif. (AP) — The LA Galaxy finished 26th in the 29-team Major League Soccer standings just one season ago, and their biggest supporters boycotted certain matches to protest a decade of poor performance.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO assassination: Retired FBI special agent details why alleged suspect name being withheld
item.description: As the hunt for the suspect who gunned down UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson continues, a retired FBI special agent details why some information about the alleged suspect is being withheld.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Five California Lottery players become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games, lottery officials announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, ABC 4

item.title: Stolen ruby slippers worn by Judy Garland in 'The Wizard of Oz' are auctioned for $28 million
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — A pair of iconic ruby slippers that were worn by Judy Garland in “The Wizard of Oz” and stolen from a museum nearly two decades ago sold for a winning bid of $28 million at auction Saturday.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI and HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 2 Pearl Harbor survivors, ages 104 and 102, return to Hawaii to honor those killed in 1941 attack
item.description: The Dec. 7, 1941, bombing killed more than 2,300 U.S. servicemen. Nearly half, or 1,177, were sailors and Marines on board the USS Arizona, which sank during the battle. The remains of more than 900 Arizona crew members are still entombed on the submerged vessel.
item.dc:creator: AUDREY McAVOY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Willy Adames agrees to $182 million, 7-year deal with the Giants, AP source says
item.description: Willy Adames has agreed to a $182 million, seven-year contract with San Francisco, providing the Giants with a power-hitting shortstop in the prime of his career, according to a person familiar with the negotiations.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Settlement offers nearly $9M to Louisiana nursing home residents kept in warehouse during hurricane
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Some of the elderly residents of seven Louisiana nursing homes who were sent in 2021 to ride out Hurricane Ida in a crowded, ill-equipped warehouse are being offered shares of a nearly $9 million settlement after they sued.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: An archbishop's knock formally restores Notre Dame to life as winds howl and heads of state look on
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Howling winds couldn’t stop Notre Dame Cathedral ’s heart from beating again. With three resounding knocks on its doors by Paris Archbishop Laurent Ulrich, wielding a specially designed crosier carved from fire-scorched beams, the monument roared back to life Saturday evening.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON and JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida prosecutor seeks to clear records of people charged with buying police-made crack in 1980s
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida prosecutor says he will seek to vacate as many as 2,600 convictions of people who bought crack cocaine manufactured by the Broward County Sheriff's Office for sting operations between 1988 and 1990.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disneyland announces closure date for Red Car Trolley attraction
item.description: Disneyland has announced a closing date for the Red Car Trolley attraction at Disney California Adventure as construction for an expansion of Avengers Campus nears.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: Five Californians become millionaires from Scratcher games
item.description: Five California Lottery players are the state’s newest millionaires after winning a combined total of $17 million from Scratcher games.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Miller High Life releases perfume that smells like a dive bar
item.description: (NEXSTAR) - If you've ever wondered how to describe the smell of your small local neighborhood bar, Miller High Life may have just provided that answer for you.
item.dc:creator: Nick Jachim, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Days after gunman killed UnitedHealthcare's CEO, police push to ID him and FBI offers reward
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The gunman who killed the CEO of the largest U.S. health insurer likely left New York City on a bus soon after the brazen ambush that has shaken corporate America, police officials said. But he left something behind: a backpack that was discovered in Central Park.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Body of missing grandmother who fell into sinkhole recovered after 4-day search
item.description: The search for 64-year-old Elizabeth Pollard, who fell into a sinkhole in Pittsburgh, has concluded after the coroner's office confirmed with news outlets that her body had been found.
item.dc:creator: Amber Baker, NewsNation

item.title: Lakefront family massacre suspect's defense demands apology for 'false' Clorox accusation
item.description: The defense team for a teen boy, charged with killing all but one member of his family in their Washington state lakefront home in October, denied accusations of crime scene tampering.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Deep dive into UnitedHealthcare CEO's personal life will play key role in investigation: retired NYPD officer
item.description: Ralph Cilento, a retired lieutenant commander at the NYPD and adjunct professor of police science at John Jay College of Criminal Justice, spoke to Fox News Digital Friday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man on trial in the killing of an Ole Miss student gave conflicting information, police say
item.description: The man on trial in the killing of University of Mississippi student Jimmie “Jay” Lee gave conflicting information to police about how well he and Lee knew each other, according to testimony Friday by an officer who helped lead the investigation.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WAGSTER PETTUS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Teen killed, naked man arrested at California High School
item.description: A 24-year-old man, possibly naked and wielding an ax, was arrested in connection with the killing of a teenager in Newbury Park on Thursday night.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, WFLA, NBC

item.title: QVC recalls 1.1 million oven gloves after more than 90 burn injury reports
item.description: QVC is recalling more than 1 million pairs of oven gloves after dozens of people were burned while using them, the U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission has announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: NASA announces further delays to Artemis moon missions
item.description: NASA is preparing to hold a news conference to discuss the agency’s Artemis program, which concerns its plans for moon launches in coming years.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Fox 5

item.title: No winner for Mega Millions, jackpot grows to $579 million
item.description: No one won the Mega Millions drawing on Tuesday night. The jackpot has now grown to an estimated $579 million ahead of the next drawing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, WGN 9
